nodes.title: Roman Gaul
nodes.description: Caesar completes the conquest of Gaul.

nodes.title: Frankish Kingdom
nodes.description: Clovis unites the Franks across former Gaul.

nodes.title: Charlemagne Crowned
nodes.description: Charlemagne is crowned Emperor of the West.

nodes.title: Norman Conquest of England
nodes.description: William, Duke of Normandy, invades England and is crowned King, tightly weaving French and English history.

nodes.title: Hundred Years' War
nodes.description: France and England fight a long dynastic war.

nodes.title: Joan of Arc at Orléans
nodes.description: Joan of Arc relieves the Siege of Orléans, turning the tide of the Hundred Years' War in France's favor.

nodes.title: Edict of Nantes
nodes.description: Henry IV signs the edict granting Calvinist Protestants (Huguenots) substantial rights, ending the French Wars of Religion.

nodes.title: Reign of Louis XIV
nodes.description: Louis XIV begins personal rule, establishing absolute monarchy and building the Palace of Versailles.

nodes.title: French Enlightenment
nodes.description: Philosophers like Voltaire, Rousseau, and Montesquieu pioneer intellectual and social reforms.

nodes.title: French Revolution
nodes.description: Revolution topples the absolute monarchy.

nodes.title: Reign of Terror
nodes.description: Radical Jacobins lead a bloody phase of the revolution, executing King Louis XVI and thousands of others.

nodes.title: Napoleonic Empire
nodes.description: Napoleon crowns himself Emperor of the French.

nodes.title: Paris Commune
nodes.description: Following defeat in the Franco-Prussian War, a revolutionary socialist government rules Paris briefly.

nodes.title: World War I
nodes.description: A continental war reshapes Europe.

nodes.title: Treaty of Versailles
nodes.description: The peace treaty ending WWI is signed in the Palace of Versailles' Hall of Mirrors, establishing the new post-war order.

nodes.title: Fall of France & Resistance
nodes.description: Nazi Germany occupies France, leading to the Vichy regime and the rise of De Gaulle's Free French Resistance.

nodes.title: Schuman Declaration
nodes.description: Foreign Minister Robert Schuman proposes pooling French and West German coal and steel, starting modern European integration.

nodes.title: Fifth Republic
nodes.description: Charles de Gaulle establishes the Fifth Republic with a strong presidency.

nodes.title: Algerian Independence
nodes.description: The Algerian War of Independence ends, marking the peak of French decolonization.

nodes.title: May 1968 Events
nodes.description: A period of civil unrest, student strikes, and wildcat general strikes shakes the government and reforms culture.

nodes.title: TGV Service Inaugurated
nodes.description: The high-speed rail network TGV is launched, revolutionizing European passenger rail travel.

nodes.title: Euro Coins & Banknotes
nodes.description: France officially retires the French franc, adopting the Euro as its sole physical currency.
